purpose: Run a non-trivial staged battery of TSA23 document-indexing delegation tests on the most recent TSA23 public TSR documents before deciding whether the document-library lane should scale.
raw_data_policy: Raw PDF text, prompts with source chunks, worker outputs, provider details, and transcripts remain under runtime/.
tracked_output_policy: Track only sanitized manifests, aggregate metrics, and supervisor conclusions.
record_budget_policy: Generated extraction tickets must not impose a hidden maximum record count. Verbose, malformed, repetitive, or low-value outputs are measured by validators and summarized as experiment results.

models.role: planned document-understanding ensemble candidate; not available in the current local Ollama catalog at wave definition time

ticket_shapes.purpose: full-document structure extraction test across all generated chunks
deterministic single-chunk structure extraction unit for orchestrated full-document coverage
strict candidate JSON extraction for field-by-field ensemble comparison

waves.goal: Extract text chunks and write sanitized chunk manifests for the three most recent selected PDFs.
waves.stop_gate: Do not contact models until manifests validate and raw text stays ignored.

waves.goal: Run qwen3-coder-next on structure_x2 for each selected document.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if outputs are parseable and preserve required document/record IDs.

waves.goal: Run qwen3-coder-next on structure_full for each selected document so every generated page-window chunk is represented before model A/B.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if full-document outputs remain parseable and do not show worse looping or format drift than Wave 1.

waves.goal: Run qwen3-coder, qwen3-coder-next, and gpt-oss:120b on identical structure_x4 tickets for one document.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if at least one model produces source-anchored parseable records without looping/tool confounds.

waves.goal: Run qwen3-coder:latest, the best Wave 2 coverage candidate, on structure_x2, structure_x4, and structure_x8 for one document.
waves.stop_gate: Compare marginal record yield, malformed-output risk, and worker token growth before scaling further.

waves.goal: Run qwen3-coder:latest once per rationale chunk so coverage is deterministic rather than dependent on one broad ticket reaching later chunks.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if every chunk returns parseable records with valid chunk IDs and repairable quote-length defects.

waves.goal: Run qwen3.6:35b-a3b-bf16 once per rationale chunk on the same seven chunk inputs used by Wave 3.1, then compare document-understanding extraction behavior against qwen3-coder:latest.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if BF16 outputs are parseable and improve or clearly characterize chunk-ID fidelity, quote discipline, and repair burden relative to Wave 3.1.

waves.goal: Repeat the best current Wave 3 cell, qwen3-coder:latest on structure_x4, three times to measure within-model consistency.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if record counts, ID preservation, and parseability are stable enough for audit.

waves.goal: Run one content_x4 ticket after structure extraction succeeds.
waves.stop_gate: Do not scale content metadata if source quotes, page anchors, or claim specificity are weak.

waves.goal: Supervisor audits a stratified sample of records with token checkpoints.
waves.stop_gate: Do not claim economics unless supervisor-token spans and worker token records are present.

waves.goal: Run two local models on the same typed TSR fact extraction ticket, produce candidate JSON from each model, then compare fields before verification.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if both candidate JSON objects parse and the comparison surface identifies agreements, missing fields, and disagreements without tracking raw source quotes.

waves.goal: Verify only the fields where the dual-model candidates disagree, are missing, or have weak provenance.
waves.stop_gate: Continue only if disagreement verification reduces paid supervisor audit burden without hiding unresolved uncertainty.

phase_pause_policy: Do not close P55 after the first successful run. Report wave results and wait for maintainer direction before moving to the next wave or closeout.
